caption: Rama and Sugriva vow friendship around the sacred fire
scene: Rama and Sugriva circle a sacred fire (agni pradakshina) to seal their vow of alliance; the vanara chiefs Hanuman, Jambavan (the noble bear), Nala and Nila stand with folded hands. Rama and Lakshmana as forest ascetics with their tall kodanda bows. Only Rama has a halo.
prompt: Traditional Tanjore (Thanjavur) style devotional painting, wide landscape 16:9: Lord Rama and the vanara (monkey) king Sugriva solemnly circling a sacred fire (agni pradakshina) together to seal their vow of alliance and friendship — walking around the flames, not shaking hands. Standing beside them with hands folded in reverence (anjali) are the vanara chiefs: Hanuman, Jambavan (a noble bear, riksha), and Nala and Nila. Rama and Lakshmana are forest ascetics — matted hair piled up (jatamukuta), bark-and-fiber garments (valkala), NO crowns or royal jewelry; each holds his great kodanda longbow — a tall warrior's bow as tall as himself, upright with tip near the ground (large, not small). Only Rama has a golden halo; Sugriva, Hanuman, and the others have NO halo. Rama is blue-skinned. Forest and mountain setting (Rishyamukha), NO palaces or cities. Rich jewel-tone colors, gold-leaf gilded ornamentation, ornate arched temple border, serene rounded figures, classical South Indian temple art, no text, no lettering.
